title: Boat Rental in Constanta: Costs, Seasons and Tips 2026
html:
A day on the water in Constanta means leaving from Tomis Marina on the Black Sea and cruising past Mamaia and the old port, with a small motorboat from around EUR 180 and crewed yachts higher. BoatHire24 lists verified boats so you can plan a Constanta boat rental with confidence.

Where to set off in Constanta

Constanta sits where the Romanian coast meets the open Black Sea, so departure points cover both calm bays and longer runs.

  • Tomis Marina: the central yacht harbour beside the old town and the Genoese Lighthouse, the usual pick-up point.
  • Mamaia: the long resort spit just north, popular for short cruises and swim stops.
  • Port of Constanta: the largest commercial harbour on the Black Sea, best admired from the water.
  • Eforie Nord: a coastal resort south of the city, a steady run of roughly 8 nautical miles down the shore.
  • Navodari and Cape Midia: the northern stretch towards the Danube Delta fringe, with quieter anchorages.

Which boat to rent in Constanta?

For a first day out, a small motorboat or a sport cruiser suits the Black Sea coast best, carrying a handful of guests on a half-day or full-day basis. The right choice depends on group size and whether you want a skipper aboard.

  1. Sport speedboat: best for couples and quick coastal hops, seats up to 8, from EUR 200 for a half-day; the Limpopo charter page shows a typical fast day boat of this kind.
  2. Small motorboat: easy family outings near Mamaia, seats up to 6, from EUR 180 for a half-day.
  3. Sailing yacht: relaxed cruising under wind, seats up to 8, from EUR 320 a day.
  4. Catamaran: roomy for groups and events, seats up to 12, from EUR 600 a day.
  5. Crewed motor yacht: a polished day with a skipper, seats up to 10, from EUR 900 a day.

How does booking a boat in Constanta work?

You choose your dates, pick a verified listing, and confirm with the operator, who handles the skipper, fuel terms and meeting point. Booking ahead matters in summer because the Romanian season is short and weekends fill quickly.

Message the operator about pick-up time, what is included, and whether fuel is extra before you pay, then read the listing terms on deposits and cancellation before confirming.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Constanta?

Prices vary with boat size, season and whether a skipper is included. When you compare rates for a yacht charter in Constanta, a shared tour, a private motorboat and a crewed yacht sit clearly apart. The bands below give a realistic guide for the 2026 season in EUR.

  • A shared coastal tour from Tomis Marina starts at roughly EUR 35 per person.
  • A small motorboat for a half-day runs from about EUR 180.
  • A catamaran for a full day costs from around EUR 600.
  • A crewed yacht for a full day starts near EUR 900.

When is the best season to sail in Constanta?

The high season runs from June to September, when the Black Sea is warm and calm enough for relaxed coastal days. Spring and autumn can be sailed too, though wind and water cool quickly once October arrives. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Constanta or list your own.

The strong northeasterly Crivat wind sweeps the Romanian coast mainly in the colder months, so for the smoothest water aim for mornings in July and August and book your day before midday when the sea is typically flattest.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 180 for a half-day motorboat to EUR 900 plus for a crewed yacht
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: half-day or full-day, roughly 4 to 8 hours
  • Capacity: small boats seat up to 8, catamarans up to 12
  • Currency: EUR
  • Departure: Tomis Marina, Constanta

Questions about boat rental in Constanta

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Constanta?

Romania requires a recognised boating qualification to skipper a powered boat yourself, but you can hire a crewed charter with no licence at all; the operator confirms the exact requirements for each listing before you book.

How many people can come aboard?

Small motorboats and speedboats typically carry up to 8 guests, while a catamaran can take up to 12, so group size is usually the first thing to settle when comparing listings.

Can I see dolphins or wildlife on the trip?

Harbour porpoises and dolphins do appear off the Romanian Black Sea coast, especially on calm summer mornings, though sightings are never guaranteed and depend entirely on conditions and luck on the day.

How far in advance should I book?

Because the Romanian summer is short, booking a week or two ahead is wise for July and August weekends, when the better boats at Tomis Marina are reserved quickly.

Browsing the BoatHire24 marketplace is the simplest way to compare verified boats, prices and dates along the Black Sea coast. Whether you want a quiet morning off Mamaia or a full day to Eforie Nord, a Constanta booking is only a few clicks away.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Constanta and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
